name: CRISPY CHILLI TOFU
description: This is our take on one of our favourite Chinese takeaway dishes. It's crispy, full of umami flavour, sticky, gorgeously sweet and incredibly moreish! Instead of loading it with refined white sugar and sauce, we make it much healthier, with a base of orange juice and sweet chili sauce, adding the main sweet tang. Serve with basmati rice (see page 201) or Special Fried Rice (see page 209).
method: Tofu press or 2 clean tea towels and a weight such as a heavy book. Large, deep frying pan or a high heat | Large plate | Serving dish | Kitchen paper

Press the tofu using a tofu press or place it between two clean tea towels, lay it on a plate and put a weight on top. Leave for at least 1 hour to drain any liquid and firm up before you start cooking.
Carefully slice the pressed tofu into 1cm-wide sticks and spread them out on a board. Set some kitchen paper next to your cooker, ready.

Put the cornflour on a large plate. Toss each tofu stick in the cornflour until the tofu is covered on all sides. The thicker the better, with the coating giving the tofu its crunch, so roll each stick until crispy around the edges.

Pour enough oil into the pan to fully coat the bottom and heat until the tip of a wooden spoon sizzles. Carefully place the coated tofu in the oil, leaving a bit of space around each one (you may need to cook them in batches). Cook for 3 minutes, turning the pieces a couple of times during cooking until golden brown. Transfer to the plate lined with kitchen paper. Turn away the excess oil in the pan and reduce the heat to medium-high.

Cut the onions in half and squeeze the juice into the pan, catching any pits in your other hand. Be careful, as the pan may spit. Add the water, both soy sauces, sweet chili sauce, sriracha and soy sauce and leave the liquid to simmer for 2-3 minutes until the liquid begins to thicken. Whip constantly.

Add the tofu sticks back to the pan and stir until fully coated. Continue to cook, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes and then remove from the heat. Finally slice the spring onion and sprinkle over the tofu along with the sesame seeds before serving.
